title: openai-responses provider: 404 on previous_response_id when store=false (default)
author: xneone
body:
## Bug Description

When using a provider configured with api: openai-responses, the gateway sends store=false or omits store, which defaults to false. On subsequent turns within the same session, the gateway includes previous_response_id referencing the previous response. Since that response was never persisted server-side, the API returns a 404.

HTTP 404: Item with id "rs_xxxxx" not found. Items are not persisted when store is set to false. Try again with store set to true, or remove this item from your input.

## Expected Behavior
The provider should not replay previous_response_id when store=false, or it should select a storage mode that makes replay safe.

## Actual Behavior
The next request fails after the first turn and the conversation cannot continue.

Related issues: #84904 and #87310.

activity.author: clawsweeper
activity.body:
Codex review: keeping this open for maintainer follow-up; there is still a little grit to resolve.

Summary
Keep this open: current main still has a plausible Responses replay failure for custom openai-responses providers because generic Responses payloads can be unstored while replaying server-owned reasoning item IDs. Direct OpenAI routes have a newer store-aware wrapper, but the reported custom proxy route is not covered by that direct-OpenAI-only path.

activity.author: tianxiaochannel-oss88
activity.body: Adding a fresh macOS/Slack data point. I can reproduce this with a custom openai-responses provider when previous_response_id is sent after an unstored first response. Removing the previous response reference makes the next request succeed, which points at replay state rather than authentication or model availability.
